title: capsella bursa-pastoris var. alpestris
intro: Capsella bursa-pastoris var. alpestris, also known as the mountain shepherd's purse, is a subspecies of the common shepherd's purse plant, characterized by its small, white flowers and heart-shaped seed pods. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The health benefits of Capsella bursa-pastoris var. alpestris include its potential to reduce menstrual cramps, improve kidney function, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for women's health issues. Capsella bursa-pastoris var. alpestris can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
